note: Individual papers mapped onto the drug-discovery flywheel: role, inputs/outputs, method (and whether it is FRO-able), the assumption each paper makes about how dementia gets cured, and how that assumption is challenged. Built by scripts/build_paperflow.py.

steps.label: Human anchor
steps.question: Which genes and loci carry causal human evidence?

steps.label: Cell state & mechanism
steps.question: Which cell state does the gene act through — and can we read it?

steps.label: Perturbation
steps.question: Does moving the target change pathology and function?

steps.label: Results & feedback
steps.question: What did the clinic teach us — which assumptions survive?

papers.title: New insights into the genetic etiology of Alzheimer's disease and related dementias.
papers.role: The field's primary human anchor — the loci everything downstream is prioritised against.
papers.inputs: 111,326 clinical / proxy AD cases and 677,663 controls.
papers.outputs: 75 risk loci (42 new), 31 prioritised genes; amyloid, tau and microglia pathways.
papers.method: Two-stage GWAS meta-analysis with gene prioritisation (L2G).
papers.fro_note: A standardised, biobank-scale locus → causal-gene → cell-type pipeline, run once and well for the whole field.
papers.assumption: Common-variant loci point to causal, druggable biology, and more loci means better targets.
papers.challenge: Links to 0 trials on this map — the anchor is enormous but inert until the downstream steps turn it into a target.

papers.title: Genome-wide meta-analysis identifies new loci and functional pathways influencing Alzheimer's disease risk.
papers.role: The prior-generation genetic anchor (IGAP) — set the target list before Bellenguez extended it.
papers.inputs: Stage-1 GWAS meta-analysis of clinically diagnosed AD plus replication.
papers.outputs: New risk loci and functional pathways: APP metabolism, tau, immunity and lipid transport.
papers.fro_note: The same locus → gene pipeline — the recurring, industrialisable step.
papers.assumption: Aggregating more cohorts steadily resolves the causal architecture.
papers.challenge: Its top pathways (amyloid, lipid) feed straight into trials that have largely failed — see the Results node.

papers.title: TREM2 lipid sensing sustains the microglial response in an Alzheimer's disease model.
papers.role: Mechanistic model — shows how a risk gene changes microglial function.
papers.inputs: TREM2 variants; an AD mouse model.
papers.outputs: TREM2 lipid sensing sustains the microglial response around plaques.
papers.method: Mouse genetics plus microglial functional assays.
papers.fro_note: Humanised microglia models with standardised functional readouts.
papers.assumption: Sustaining the microglial response is protective.
papers.challenge: Whether more microglial response preserves cognition is untested here — the phenotype gap.

papers.title: Human and mouse single-nucleus transcriptomics reveal TREM2-dependent and TREM2-independent cellular responses in Alzheimer's disease.
papers.role: Cell-state map — turns a risk gene (TREM2) into a disease-associated cell state.
papers.inputs: Anchored genes (TREM2); 5xFAD mice and human AD brain.
papers.outputs: TREM2-dependent DAM confirmed; a new Serpina3n+C4b+ reactive oligodendrocyte state; TREM2-independent human responses.
papers.fro_note: Atlas-scale, standardised human snRNA / spatial plus a validated disease-model panel, so states are comparable across labs.
papers.assumption: Disease-associated cell states are causal and actionable, and mouse states transfer to human.
papers.challenge: Its own TREM2-independent human finding — plus work arguing DAM is partly reactive/downstream rather than driving.

papers.title: Alzheimer's Disease: An Updated Overview of Its Genetics.
papers.role: The node that actually reaches the clinic — familial genetics to amyloid-targeting trials.
papers.inputs: Familial (APP / PSEN1 / PSEN2) and sporadic AD genetics.
papers.outputs: Links to real trials — γ-secretase modulators/inhibitors (tarenflurbil, semagacestat, avagacestat, begacestat).
papers.fro_note: A synthesis, not a method to accelerate.
papers.assumption: Familial APP/PSEN biology generalises to sporadic AD, and amyloid-lowering modifies disease.
papers.challenge: Every drug it links to failed or was halted — the Results node closes the loop against this assumption.

papers.title: Rare coding variants in PLCG2, ABI3, and TREM2 implicate microglial-mediated innate immunity in Alzheimer's disease.
papers.role: Rare-variant anchor — pins causality more tightly than common variants can.
papers.inputs: Rare coding-variant analysis across AD case-control cohorts.
papers.outputs: Rare variants in PLCG2, ABI3 and TREM2 implicate microglial innate immunity causally.
papers.fro_note: Large-scale sequencing plus burden testing — standardisable infrastructure.
papers.assumption: Rare high-impact variants reveal the causal cell type (microglia).
papers.challenge: Names microglia as causal, but which microglial state to move is only answered at the mechanism node.

papers.title: Inhibiting the NLRP3 inflammasome with MCC950 promotes non-phlogistic clearance of amyloid-β and cognitive function in APP/PS1 mice.
papers.role: Perturbation — an actual intervention experiment, not an association.
papers.inputs: APP/PS1 mice; the NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor MCC950.
papers.outputs: Non-phlogistic amyloid-β clearance and improved cognition in the model.
papers.method: Small-molecule inhibition in a disease model with a behavioural readout.
papers.fro_note: Target-specific medicinal chemistry — a discovery bet more than an infrastructure gap.
papers.assumption: Dampening one innate-immune node clears pathology and rescues function.
papers.challenge: Mouse cognitive rescue is a weak predictor of human benefit — the trial node is where this breaks.

papers.title: Single-cell epigenomic analyses implicate candidate causal variants at inherited risk loci for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases.
papers.role: Regulatory fine-map — assigns GWAS risk to specific variants, cell types and putative target genes.
papers.inputs: Single-cell ATAC-seq of human brain; AD and PD GWAS loci.
papers.outputs: Candidate causal variants at inherited risk loci mapped to cell-type-specific regulatory elements.
papers.fro_note: Brain-wide, cell-type-resolved regulatory atlases — highly FRO-able.
papers.assumption: Chromatin accessibility pinpoints the causal variant and cell type.
papers.challenge: Accessibility is not causation; the perturbation node still has to prove the variant does something.

papers.title: A common haplotype lowers PU.1 expression in myeloid cells and delays onset of Alzheimer's disease.
papers.role: The shared node made concrete — a regulatory variant → transcription-factor dose → cell state → disease timing.
papers.inputs: Survival GWAS (14,406 cases / 25,849 controls); LDSC over 220 cell types; myeloid eQTL.
papers.outputs: rs1057233(G) lowers SPI1 / PU.1 in monocytes and macrophages and delays AD onset — a dose-tunable, directional hypothesis.
papers.method: Survival GWAS + cell-type LDSC + eQTL + myeloid follow-up.
papers.fro_note: Systematic enhancer / TF-dosage mapping in human microglia (MPRA + CRISPRi tuning) — a clean FRO candidate.
papers.assumption: A transcription factor's dose is causal and tunable, and nudging cell state changes disease trajectory.
papers.challenge: The synaptic camp: regulating microglia need not preserve synapses or cognition; and the causal direction is unproven.

papers.title: In 2024, the amyloid-cascade-hypothesis still remains a working hypothesis, no less but certainly no more.
papers.role: Feedback — a paper whose job is to interrogate the field's central assumption.
papers.inputs: The full arc of amyloid trials and biomarker data.
papers.outputs: Verdict: the amyloid cascade remains a working hypothesis, 'no less but certainly no more.'
papers.assumption: The evidence base does not yet justify amyloid certainty.
papers.challenge: This is the challenge — it down-weights the amyloid-lowering targets that dominate the Trials node.

papers.title: The new genetic landscape of Alzheimer's disease: from amyloid cascade to genetically driven synaptic failure hypothesis?
papers.role: Feedback that re-ranks the theories — argues genetics pushes the field from amyloid cascade toward synaptic failure.
papers.outputs: A reframing: genetically driven synaptic failure as the organising hypothesis.
papers.assumption: The locus set points at synaptic biology more than amyloid production.
papers.challenge: Directly questions the amyloid node and reinforces the synaptic anchor — the loop updating itself.

edges.note: Argues genetics favours synaptic failure over the amyloid cascade.
